steps.title: Weigh the study pattern
steps.content: The Brazil study did not find the usual positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury. But several studies outside North America, including the Swedish study, matched the North American pattern, while few matched the Brazil result.

steps.title: Choose the typical pattern
steps.content: A is contradicted because the association appears outside North America. B invents unusually high levels in Brazil. D attacks the Swedish study's measurement accuracy without evidence. The broader evidence suggests the positive association is typical. The answer is C.
